hint: In Toki Pona, the adjective always comes AFTER the noun. 'Person' + 'Good'.
Think: 'Water' + 'Hot'. Modifiers follow the main word.
How do you say 'Room of Knowledge'?
When 'mi' or 'sina' is the subject, you just put the verb right after.
If the subject is NOT 'mi' or 'sina', you MUST put 'li' before the verb.
Subject (Animal Big) + 'li' + Verb (Wait/Stay). 'awen' means to stay or wait, not to sleep.
Put 'e' right before the thing being acted upon (the direct object).

prompt: The good person makes a house.
hint: Putting it all together: Subject (Person Good) + 'li' + Verb (Make) + 'e' + Object (House).
